query:
#337 — The single-line fix is right, but the regression test is           
  non-negotiable. Without it, a future config change can silently
  reintroduce the invalid merged weight sum and nothing catches it. The     
  specific test case (w_sim=0.6 + w_nli=0.6 → Err) is exactly the right
  boundary condition.

  #345 — The architect finding two additional sites the investigator missed 
  is the process working correctly. The four-change scope is the right
  scope. One thing to verify: confirm search.rs:1040 and session.rs:406 use 
  the same u32 type — the saturating fold only makes sense if the 
  accumulator type matches.

  #346 — Tight and correct. The "no legitimate caller broken" argument is   
  solid given SessionRegister already enforces the same guard. No concerns.
                                                                            
  #378+#379 — This is the one that needs attention before delivery starts.  
  The REWORK verdict means the delivery agent must work from the architect's
   revised table, not the investigator's original report. Twelve call sites 
  with two distinct helpers. One thing to verify: the             
  render_knowledge_reuse:1013-1017 row in the table shows a truncated
  function name (.replace('|'...) — confirm the full escape_md_cell call is
  in the revised design doc, not just the table summary.

  The leading-# rule is critical and easy to get wrong. A global            
  replace('#', "\\#") would corrupt every issue reference in every cycle
  review output. The "only when string starts with #" constraint must be    
  explicit in the implementation note.                            

  #380 — Flag this clearly to the delivery agent: this is a behavior change,
   not just a comment fix. as i64 wrapping → try_from().unwrap_or(i64::MAX)
  saturating is a semantic difference that affects far-future timestamps.   
  The comment correction is secondary. The test coverage question is: is
  there an existing test that exercises this path with a large obs.ts? If
  not, add one.

  Ready to deliver. The REWORK on #378+#379 is the only gate — confirm the  
  revised design doc is authoritative before the delivery agent picks it up.

entries.title: Pool selection for read-only context_status aggregates: always check compute_status_aggregates as direct precedent
merge_configs: per-file validation does not cover cross-level invariant violations; always re-validate the merged result
Weight budget assignment when one of two planned scoring terms is deferred to a later feature
Test plan expected values copied from a different input set silently fail when inputs differ
Numeric constant mismatch across SPEC / pseudocode / test-plan causes latent test-vs-implementation conflict
